frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Arre bhai, har aadmi ko shak ki nazar se dekhna bhi theek nahi hai. Kuch galat log hote hain, lekin iska matlab yeh thodi ki sab mard kharab hain; main khud school mein itne saal se padhata hoon, kabhi kisi ko pareshan nahi kiya. Aajkal ladkiyan pehle se hi maan leti hain ki saamne wala aadmi dangerous hoga, yeh bhi anyay hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear and reframes their safety behavior as unfair prejudice against men. It encodes the 'not all men' response, centers the speaker's own goodness, and challenges women's wariness as irrational.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya is 27 and works as a software engineer in Pune. She is doing well professionally and seems happy. But she is not thinking about marriage and when you bring it up she changes the subject. You are sitting with your wife after dinner. Priya has gone to bed. You are telling your wife what you think needs to happen and what you are going to do about it and what it will mean for Priya if she waits until she is 30 and what people in your town are already saying about a 27 year old unmarried working woman.
